canonical_definition: Across the provided sources, FineWeb-Edu is characterized as a large, curated dataset of high-quality educational text for language models. The dataset is derived from CommonCrawl and is identified as containing English text. The curation process is described as involving LLM-based annotation to assess the "educational value" of documents. Its scale is represented differently across papers, with one source citing "10 billion tokens" and another mentioning "approximately 450,000" annotated documents. The documented uses for FineWeb-Edu include training language models and serving as a source for data sampling, while one definition also notes its role in evaluation.
papers: Multimodal Fine-grained Context Interaction Graph Modeling for Conversational Speech Synthesis
Disentangling and Integrating Relational and Sensory Information in Transformer Architectures
Joint MoE Scaling Laws: Mixture of Experts Can Be Memory Efficient
Organize the Web: Constructing Domains Enhances Pre-Training Data Curation
Programming Every Example: Lifting Pre-training Data Quality Like Experts at Scale

definitions.text: A large, curated dataset of high-quality educational text from the web, used for training and evaluating language models.
definitions.paper: Disentangling and Integrating Relational and Sensory Information in Transformer Architectures

definitions.text: An English dataset annotated by a large language model, containing approximately 450,000 documents assessed for educational value, used as a starting point for sampling in this study.
definitions.paper: Multimodal Fine-grained Context Interaction Graph Modeling for Conversational Speech Synthesis
